---
title: "Mathematical and Search Substrates"
chapter_id: "mathematical-and-search-substrates"
part_id: "routing-compression-representation-substrates"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "genesiscode"
  - "temporal_coil_research"
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "treellm"
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "coilmoecot"
  - "circle_calculus_core"
  - "circle_ai_architectures"
  - "proof_carrying_circular_computation"
  - "theseus_circle_transfer"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Mathematical and Search Substrates

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `mathematical-and-search-substrates` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Representation, and Substrates |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `genesiscode`, `temporal_coil_research`, `cognitive_compilation`, `treellm`, `simulation_scaling`, `coilmoecot`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_architectures`, `proof_carrying_circular_computation`, `theseus_circle_transfer` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The source corpus contains exploratory mathematical/search substrates that may matter but must not be overclaimed.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Novel substrates can become authority theater if they are not tied to baselines, adoption gates, and falsification criteria.

## Core Claim

[mathematical-and-search-substrates.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Coils, calculi, and geometric search belong in the stack as optional specialist substrates until tests show where they improve search, routing, or compression.

## Mechanism

- Frame each substrate as a specialist core or IR option.
- Define expected advantage, baseline, and falsification condition.
- Route experiments through evidence gates before adoption.

## Interfaces

- Routing treats substrate as specialist.
- Compression tests representation efficiency.
- Evidence compares against baselines.

## Invariants

- Exploratory claims stay exploratory.
- Baselines are recorded.
- Failed hypotheses remain visible.

## Failure modes

- Performance overclaiming.
- Opaque math treated as proof.
- Adoption without regression tests.

## Minimal implementation

A technical-substrate appendix plan with experiment matrix and adoption gates.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Baseline comparison test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Representation efficiency test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Falsification review | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. |
| `temporal_coil_research` | Temporal Coil Research | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Manifest-driven A/B process for temporal-coil integration in language model training. |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. |
| `treellm` | TreeLLM | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Hierarchical semantic token system for grounded, efficient, explainable language modeling. |
| `simulation_scaling` | Simulation Scaling Law | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Resource constraints on scope, clockspeed, and fidelity in simulations. |
| `coilmoecot` | CoilMoECOT Whitepaper v2.0 | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Prime-temporal coil networks as governed specialist cores in MoECOT runtime. |
| `circle_calculus_core` | Circle Calculus | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Proof-carrying finite cyclic mathematics project with Lean proofs, Python reference models, Rust utilities, theorem manifests, papers, and Quarto living book. |
| `circle_ai_architectures` | Circle AI Architectures | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Disciplined Circle AI thesis: use phase, recurrence, rotation, sparse cyclic mixing, circular memory, harmonic transforms, or geometry-aware structure only where the structure is real and baselines support it. |
| `proof_carrying_circular_computation` | Proof-Carrying Circular Computation | Planned use from inventory/manifest: CoilIR-style path from circle/coil expressions to dictionary-recognized cyclic structure, Lean-proved rewrite/address transformations, backend selection, and benchmark validation. |
| `theseus_circle_transfer` | Theseus Circle Calculus Transfer Lane | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Report-only bridge from Circle finite fixtures into private Theseus benchmark design with explicit quality/runtime/memory/transfer/failure-case claim boundaries. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
